# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder:30b)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Process
on:
  push:
    branches:
      - main
      - master
permissions:
  contents: write
  packages: write
  pages: write
  id-token: write
  deployments: write
  issues: write
  pull-requests: write
  repository-projects: write
  security-events: write
  actions: write
jobs:
  build-and-publish:
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Setup Node.js
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '18'
          registry-url: 'https://registry.npmjs.org'
      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci
      - name: Build packages
        run: npm run build
      - name: Publish to npm
        run: npm publish
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }}
  create-release:
    needs: build-and-publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Create Release
        uses: actions/create-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          tag_name: v${{ github.run_number }}
          release_name: Release ${{ github.run_number }}
          body: Automated release
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
  update-documentation:
    needs: build-and-publish
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
      - name: Update documentation
        run: |
          echo "Updating documentation..."
          # Add documentation update commands here
      - name: Commit and push documentation changes
        run: |
          git config --local user.email "action@github.com"
          git config --local user.name "GitHub Action"
          git add .
          git commit -m "Update documentation" || true
          git push origin HEAD:${{ github.ref }} || true
